raw_title: Misogyny as a Structural Hub: Unveiling a Mode of Opportunity Structure for Chinese Alternative Communities
raw_abstract: This study argues that in China’s digital sphere, online misogyny functions less as a gateway to extremism and more as a structural hub for systemic discontent. Drawing on opportunity structure theory, our mixed-methods approach combines computational analysis of posts from Baidu Tieba ( n = 91,026) with in-depth interviews ( n = 27). We find that “Gendered Insults” exhibits the highest betweenness centrality, acting as a structural bridge connecting disparate frustrations. Women-targeting topics are both speakable and resonant within the community, anchored by a self-deprecating “sewer mouse” masculinity that navigates opportunity structures. Interviews reveal that when political opportunity structures remain closed while discursive and mediated (legacy platform) opportunities stay relatively accessible, misogyny becomes a functional language for channeling widespread socioeconomic anxieties. We conclude that in this context, misogyny operates not as a starting point for radicalization but as a core organizing principle for the socially disillusioned. This suggests that divergent structural conditions produce different mechanisms.
title_en: Misogyny as a Structural Hub: Unveiling a Mode of Opportunity Structure for Chinese Alternative Communities
title_zh: 厌女症作为结构性枢纽：揭示中国另类社区的机会结构模式
abstract_en: This study argues that in China’s digital sphere, online misogyny functions less as a gateway to extremism and more as a structural hub for systemic discontent. Drawing on opportunity structure theory, our mixed-methods approach combines computational analysis of posts from Baidu Tieba ( n = 91,026) with in-depth interviews ( n = 27). We find that “Gendered Insults” exhibits the highest betweenness centrality, acting as a structural bridge connecting disparate frustrations. Women-targeting topics are both speakable and resonant within the community, anchored by a self-deprecating “sewer mouse” masculinity that navigates opportunity structures. Interviews reveal that when political opportunity structures remain closed while discursive and mediated (legacy platform) opportunities stay relatively accessible, misogyny becomes a functional language for channeling widespread socioeconomic anxieties. We conclude that in this context, misogyny operates not as a starting point for radicalization but as a core organizing principle for the socially disillusioned. This suggests that divergent structural conditions produce different mechanisms.
abstract_zh: 本研究认为，在中国的数字领域，网络厌女症较少作为极端主义的入口，而更多作为系统性不满的结构性枢纽。借鉴机会结构理论，我们采用混合方法，结合对百度贴吧帖子（n = 91,026）的计算分析和深度访谈（n = 27）。我们发现，“性别侮辱”表现出最高的中介中心性，作为连接不同挫折的结构性桥梁。针对女性的主题在社区内既可说又具共鸣，由一种自嘲的“下水道老鼠”男性气质锚定，这种气质在机会结构中导航。访谈揭示，当政治机会结构保持封闭，而话语和媒介（传统平台）机会相对可及，厌女症成为疏导广泛社会经济焦虑的功能性语言。我们得出结论，在此背景下，厌女症并非激进化的起点，而是社会幻灭者的核心组织原则。这表明不同的结构条件产生不同的机制。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了中国网络社区中厌女言论的作用。研究发现，这些言论不是导致极端主义的起点，而是人们表达各种不满的“连接点”。通过分析大量网络帖子和访谈，研究者发现，当人们无法通过政治途径表达不满，但能在网上讨论时，厌女言论成为一种发泄社会经济焦虑的方式。这帮助社区成员组织起来，应对社会失望。
plain_summary_en: This study examines the role of misogynistic speech in Chinese online communities. It finds that such speech is not a starting point for extremism but serves as a 'hub' for expressing various frustrations. By analyzing many online posts and conducting interviews, researchers discovered that when people cannot voice discontent through political channels but can discuss it online, misogyny becomes a way to vent socioeconomic anxieties. This helps community members organize in response to social disillusionment.
research_direction_zh: 该研究属于数字社会学和传播学领域，关注网络社区中的性别暴力、社会不满的结构性表达，以及机会结构理论在数字环境中的应用。
research_direction_en: This research falls within digital sociology and communication studies, focusing on gender-based violence in online communities, the structural expression of social discontent, and the application of opportunity structure theory in digital environments.
